blockingIssues.description: `src/model/data_builder.py` can leak earlier same-day games into later same-day rows because it appends finished games back into in-memory team histories while stamping every row with a prior-day `as_of_timestamp`.

The training-data builder does not assemble the actual t9-t15 feature outputs; it substitutes prior-year snapshot proxies and hard-coded pitching, bullpen, and weather constants instead of the requested engineered feature set.

`src/model/stacking.py` computes holdout Brier metrics but still saves stacked artifacts even when the stacked model regresses versus the base XGBoost model on the normal CLI/default path, violating the feature's own `stacked_brier <= base_brier` contract.
CLI-generated stacking bundles are joblib-pickled as `__main__.StackingEnsembleModel` and fail to reload in a fresh interpreter, so the saved stacking model is not reliably reusable across processes.

`src/backtest/walk_forward.py` resolves one cached training dataset and slices it per window instead of recomputing features at each walk-forward step, so it does not satisfy the explicit per-step anti-leakage recomputation requirement.

The walk-forward backtest defaults to `calibration_method="identity"` while `src/model/calibration.py` now defaults to `platt`, so the shipped backtest CLI no longer evaluates the same calibrated inference path used by the current ML pipeline.

appliedUpdates.description: Added `pipeline.build_training_data`, `pipeline.train_xgboost`, and `pipeline.train_stacking` so the shared services manifest exposes the ml-pipeline training entrypoints surfaced during scrutiny.

Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` to replace the stale isotonic-only pipeline description with the current probability-calibration stage and Platt-default note used by the verified 2025 holdout path.

Refreshed `.factory/library/ml-calibration.md` with the current configurable calibration methods, passing 2025 holdout metrics, and the `python -m` joblib serialization convention used by calibration bundles.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Split the manual-verification guidance by ML feature type instead of requiring the same subset-train plus short-backtest recipe for every task. Data-builder features should allow direct dataset build/integrity checks, and calibration fixes should require the relevant holdout evaluation that matches their acceptance gates.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `t16-training-data-builder` and `t19-isotonic-calibration` both recorded justified deviations because the current `ml-worker` manual-verification step is too generic for their feature contracts.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the repo's Windows-safe startup/validation workflow in the ML worker procedure by pointing workers to `.factory/services.yaml` `*_windows` commands or direct `.venv\Scripts\python.exe` invocations when `.factory/init.sh` or shell-agnostic helpers fail.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for `t18-lr-stacking` and `t19-isotonic-calibration` both cite justified setup deviations because the existing ML skill does not incorporate the Windows guidance already captured in `.factory/library/environment.md`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a shared rule that downstream ML/backtest features must default to the currently verified calibration path from `src.model.calibration` unless the feature explicitly says otherwise, so backtests and later pipeline stages stay aligned with the live calibrated inference path.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `t19-isotonic-calibration` switched the verified default to Platt scaling, but `t20-walk-forward-backtest` still defaulted to `identity`, creating immediate backtest-to-inference drift.

rejectedObservations.observation: The `init_windows` quoting bug in `.factory/services.yaml` and bare-`pip` use in `.factory/init.sh`.
rejectedObservations.reason: real issue, but fixing it would require overwriting existing shared startup entries rather than the additive-only updates permitted during scrutiny synthesis

rejectedObservations.observation: Repeated calibration-state ambiguity observations across `t19-isotonic-calibration` and `t20-walk-forward-backtest`.
rejectedObservations.reason: addressed by the applied library updates to `.factory/library/architecture.md` and `.factory/library/ml-calibration.md`
